home.heroSubtitleBefore: ARC Prize Foundation is a nonprofit advancing
home.heroSubtitleAfter: artificial general intelligence research through benchmarks & prizes.
home.benchmarksDesc1: We build scientifically-grounded benchmarks that reveal the gap between what's easy for humans, hard for AI.
home.benchmarksDesc2: ARC-AGI has repeatedly identified key inflection points in AI progress, including the emergence of reasoning systems and the rise of capable AI agents.
home.newIdeasTitle: New ideas still needed for AGI.
home.newIdeasDesc1: While AI has made tremendous progress, ARC-AGI shows us scaling alone will not reach AGI.
home.newIdeasDesc2: Our mission is simple: guide research, accelerate progress, and ensure the path to AGI remains open and accessible to all.
home.newIdeasDesc3: We define AGI as a system that can match the learning efficiency of humans.
home.arcAgi3Intro: Introducing ARC-AGI-3. The world's only unbeaten benchmark that measures agentic intelligence.
home.leaderboardDesc: Trusted by the world's leading AI labs and top academic researchers, explore the only benchmark that measures fluid intelligence. It's easy for humans, hard for AI.
home.prizeDesc: Get rewarded for open sourcing novel progress. In partnership with Kaggle, over $2M is up for grabs.
home.founderQuote: "AGI is the most important technology humanity will create, and we believe it is achievable in our lifetime. But ARC shows us we still need new ideas. Maybe they'll come from you?"

about.title: ARC Prize Foundation
about.subtitle: A North Star for AGI
about.p1: The ARC Prize Foundation is a non-profit dedicated to accelerating the development of Artificial General Intelligence (AGI). We believe that true AGI requires more than just scaling up existing AI models. It demands a fundamental shift towards systems capable of genuine fluid intelligence, the ability to adapt to new situations and solve novel problems efficiently, much like humans do.
about.p2: Our core mission is to identify, measure, and ultimately close the capability gap between human and artificial intelligence on tasks that are simple for people yet remain difficult for even the most advanced AI systems today.
about.p3: We achieve this by creating and curating human-calibrated benchmarks that serve as a clear and objective measure of progress towards AGI.
about.p4: These benchmarks are not just tests, they are a driving force for AI research. They guide researchers towards the most important bottlenecks on the way to AGI, and incentivize them to explore approaches that go beyond pattern matching and memorization.
about.p5: The ARC Prize Foundation cultivates a collaborative, open-source ecosystem, encouraging the broad sharing of ideas and solutions to overcome the key limitations of current AI.
about.p6: Ultimately, we envision a future where AI possesses the adaptable, creative problem-solving abilities necessary to benefit humanities most pressing challenges.
about.p7Before: If you'd like to get involved, please visit our

competitions.subtitle2026: $2M in prizes. 3 tracks. Open source progress toward AGI.
competitions.arcAgi3Desc: Interactive reasoning benchmark. Prizes for top-performing AI agents.
competitions.arcAgi2Desc: Static reasoning benchmark. Prizes for top scores on ARC-AGI-2.
competitions.paperPrizeDesc: Awards for papers that advance our understanding of how to achieve strong performance on ARC-AGI.
competitions.goal1: Increase the number of AI researchers exploring new ideas
competitions.spiritP1: All leading participants are expected to open source their solutions to be eligible for a prize.
competitions.spiritP2: The primary mission of ARC Prize is to accelerate progress toward open Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) by making cutting-edge solutions freely available to the entire research community.
competitions.spiritP3: We provide substantial prize money, dedicated infrastructure, and public recognition to incentivize and reward participants who share reproducible methods openly. While anyone is welcome to use our public data and leaderboards independently, the privilege of competing officially - and benefiting from our private evaluations, global visibility, and chance to win prizes - comes with a commitment to transparency and openness.
competitions.licenseDesc: In order for a submission to be eligible, all code and methods authored by the submitter must be made open source under a permissive public domain license (eg. CC0 or MIT-0). Additionally, any 3rd party code or methods not authored by the submitter must be available under, at least, an open source license which allows public sharing (eg. Apache-2.0, GPLv3).
competitions.eligibilityP1: Prizes are awarded at the sole discretion of ARC Prize Inc. and are subject to review by our Technical Team. We may issue more or fewer awards based on the spirit of the prize and the received submissions.
competitions.eligibilityP2: Participants must open source their solutions before receiving official private evaluation scores. This applies across all three competition tracks.
competitions.general1: All prizes require reproducible, open-source submissions
competitions.general2: Solutions must be submitted through the designated Kaggle competition for each track
competitions.general3: Internet access is not available during Kaggle evaluation (no API-based systems like GPT/Claude/etc.)
competitions.general4: Detailed rules for each track are available on their respective pages

leaderboard.title: Leaderboard
leaderboard.understandingP1: ARC-AGI has evolved from its first versions (ARC-AGI-1 and 2) which measured passive fluid intelligence, to ARC-AGI-3 which challenges AI agents to adapt on the fly to novel interactive environments.
leaderboard.understandingP2: The scatter plot above visualizes the critical relationship between cost-per-task and performance - a key measure of efficiency. True intelligence isn't just about solving problems, but solving them efficiently with minimal resources.
leaderboard.note1: Only systems which required less than $10,000 to run are shown.
leaderboard.note2: For models that were not able to produce full test outputs, remaining tasks were marked as incorrect.
leaderboard.note3: Results marked as "preview" are unofficial and may be based on incomplete testing.

tasks.title: Tasks
tasks.description: Browse all tasks in the ARC-AGI benchmark series. Each task tests an AI system's ability to generalize and reason from limited examples.

arcAgi.title: ARC-AGI Series
arcAgi.subtitle: Benchmarks for General Intelligence
arcAgi.measureDesc: In 2019, François Chollet published the paper "On the Measure of Intelligence" which established the theoretical foundation for the ARC-AGI benchmark.
arcAgi.measureQuote: It defined intelligence as skill-acquisition efficiency on unknown tasks. Simply put: how quickly can you learn new skills?
arcAgi.definingAgiP1: To make deliberate progress towards more intelligent and human-like systems, we need to be following an appropriate feedback signal. We need to rigorously define and measure intelligence.
arcAgi.definingAgiP2: There exists a gap between AI and humans. Today, AI can automate many valuable tasks, but only humans are capable of open-ended innovation and discovery. Our mission is to continually study frontier AI progress and create useful and scientifically grounded benchmarks to highlight the remaining gap.
arcAgi.definingAgiP3: One popular definition of AGI, "a system that can automate the majority of economically valuable work," while a useful goal, is an incorrect measure of intelligence.
arcAgi.definingAgiP4: Measuring task-specific skill is not a good proxy for intelligence.
arcAgi.definingAgiP5: Skill is heavily influenced by prior knowledge and experience. Unlimited priors or unlimited training data allows developers to "buy" levels of skill for a system. This masks a system's own generalization power.
arcAgi.definingAgiP6: Chollet argued in the paper that skill itself is not a good measure of intelligence because it conflates priors and generalization ability.
arcAgi.definingAgiP7: The ability to acquire skills, abstractions, and reason generally are pre-requisites of an intelligence which can ultimately accelerate innovation.
arcAgi.definingAgiP8: The ARC Prize definition of AGI
arcAgi.definingAgiP9: AGI is a system that can match the learning efficiency of humans.
arcAgi.definingAgiFormal: A system S, relative to priors P, is generally intelligent if S turns its experience and priors into new skills at valuable tasks that involve uncertainty and adaptation at a rate comparable to humans, on the task distribution reachable from P.
arcAgi.definingAgiP11: This means that a system is able to adapt to new problems it has not seen before and that its creators (developers) did not anticipate.
arcAgi.definingAgiP12: ARC-AGI is the only AI benchmark that measures our progress towards general intelligence.
arcAgi.coreKnowledgeP1: ARC-AGI is designed to rely only on cognitive abilities that humans are born with — that is, "core knowledge."
arcAgi.coreKnowledgeP2: Core knowledge includes basic concepts about objects, agents, space, time, numbers, and more — common sense that any human (regardless of culture) shares.
arcAgi.coreKnowledgeP3: By limiting prior knowledge to core knowledge, ARC-AGI ensures the benchmark measures genuine generalization and reasoning ability, not pattern matching relying on massive pre-training data.
arcAgi.coreKnowledgeP4: This is the key distinction between ARC-AGI and most other AI benchmarks.
arcAgi.coreKnowledgeP5: If a benchmark included, for instance, tasks involving written English, it would immediately disadvantage any AI that hadn't been extensively pre-trained on vast text corpora. It would also disadvantage humans that did not know English. English, or any language, is a cultural artifact, not a measure of inherent cognitive ability.
arcAgi.coreKnowledgeP6: The resources can come in two forms:
arcAgi.coreKnowledgeLi1: Prior knowledge: knowledge built into the system at design time (architecture, weights, etc.)
arcAgi.coreKnowledgeLi2: Experience: knowledge gained by the system at runtime from task interactions
arcAgi.coreKnowledgeP7: That is to say, intelligence is the rate at which a learner turns its experience and priors into new skills at valuable tasks that involve uncertainty and adaptation.
arcAgi.designSubtitle: Easy for Humans, Hard for AI
arcAgi.designP1: At the core of ARC-AGI benchmark design is the principle of "Easy for Humans, Hard for AI."
arcAgi.designP2: The human mind is our only existence proof of general intelligence. As such, we set it as our reference point to judge AI.
arcAgi.designP3: Many AI benchmarks measure performance on tasks that require extensive training or specialized knowledge (Ph.D.-level problems). ARC Prize focuses instead on tasks that humans solve effortlessly yet AI finds challenging which highlight fundamental gaps in AI's reasoning and adaptability.
arcAgi.designP4: This approach reveals the essential qualities of intelligence - such as the ability to generalize from limited examples, synthesize symbolic rules, and flexibly apply known concepts in novel contexts - that current AI systems struggle to replicate.
arcAgi.designP5: By emphasizing these human-intuitive tasks, we not only measure progress more clearly but also inspire researchers to pursue genuinely novel ideas, moving beyond incremental improvements toward meaningful breakthroughs.

common.noSpam: No spam. You can unsubscribe at anytime.
common.getStartedUpdates: Get started and receive official contest updates and news.
common.subscribeUpdates: Subscribe to get started and receive official contest updates and news.

donate.title: Support ARC Prize
donate.p1: The ARC Prize Foundation is a nonprofit dedicated to accelerating the development of artificial general intelligence. Your donation directly supports benchmark development, competition prizes, and the open-source community.
donate.p2: Every contribution helps us build more scientific measures of AGI, incentivize researchers to explore new ideas beyond scaling, and ensure the path to AGI remains open and accessible to all.
donate.p3: Thank you for joining us in pushing the frontier of AGI research.

history.title: History
history.p1: The story of ARC-AGI began in 2019 with François Chollet's paper "On the Measure of Intelligence," which redefined how we measure intelligence, defining it as skill-acquisition efficiency on unknown tasks.
history.p2: From the original ARC-AGI-1 to ARC-AGI-2 and now ARC-AGI-3, the benchmark has continuously evolved, always focusing on revealing the core gap between AI and human intelligence: fluid intelligence.
history.p3: Since launching in 2024, the ARC Prize competition has attracted hundreds of teams worldwide, catalyzing multiple breakthrough open-source solutions.

jobs.title: Jobs
jobs.p1: The ARC Prize Foundation is looking for talented individuals who share our mission of advancing artificial general intelligence research. We are a small, focused team that values openness, collaboration, and scientific rigor.
jobs.p2: We currently have no open positions, but we welcome you to send your resume to team@arcprize.org and we will keep in touch.

research.title: Research
research.p1: The ARC Prize Foundation is committed to advancing the scientific understanding of general intelligence. Our research focuses on building benchmarks that rigorously define and measure intelligence, revealing the fundamental limitations of AI systems in fluid reasoning and adaptability.
research.p2: Through the ARC-AGI benchmark series, we have repeatedly identified key inflection points in AI progress, including the emergence of reasoning systems and the rise of capable AI agents. These findings point the research community toward the breakthroughs still needed for AGI.
research.p3: We encourage researchers to explore new paradigms beyond scaling and to open-source their methods and findings to accelerate progress across the field.

partners.title: Partners
partners.p1: The ARC Prize Foundation partners with leading AI labs, academic institutions, and technology platforms worldwide to advance the frontier of AGI research.
partners.p2: Our partners include Kaggle (competition platform), top AI labs, and academic research teams. To explore partnership opportunities, contact team@arcprize.org.

platform.title: Platform
platform.p1: The ARC Prize platform provides a unified entry point for researchers and competition participants to access benchmark data, leaderboards, submission systems, and community resources.
platform.p2: Through our Kaggle-hosted competition system, participants can submit solutions, receive real-time evaluation results, and engage with the global research community.

community.title: Community
community.p1: The ARC Prize community brings together researchers, developers, and enthusiasts from around the world who are passionate about AGI. We believe open collaboration is the most effective way to accelerate scientific progress.
community.p2: Join our Discord server to participate in discussions, share ideas, get the latest competition updates, and connect with other members.

events.title: Events
events.p1: The ARC Prize Foundation regularly hosts online and in-person events, including competition launches, milestone announcements, result reveals, and academic workshops.
events.p2: Follow us on Discord and social media for the latest event information.

resources.title: Resources
resources.p1: Explore research resources provided by ARC Prize, including benchmark documentation, API access, dataset downloads, paper citations, and tutorials.
resources.p2: Whether you're new to ARC-AGI or an experienced researcher, these resources will help you get started quickly and deepen your understanding of the benchmark's design philosophy and evaluation methodology.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.p1: The ARC Prize Foundation values your privacy. The information we collect is used solely to improve your experience and advance our mission.
privacy.p2: We may collect information you voluntarily provide (such as your email address) to send competition updates and newsletters. You can unsubscribe at any time.
privacy.p3: We do not sell, trade, or otherwise transfer your personally identifiable information to outside parties.
privacy.p4: To learn more or request deletion of your data, contact team@arcprize.org.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.p1: By using the ARC Prize website and participating in competitions, you agree to the following terms.
terms.p2: All competition submissions must comply with the rules of their respective tracks, including open-source licensing requirements and eligibility conditions.
terms.p3: ARC Prize Inc. reserves the right to modify competition rules and prize structures at any time.

policy.title: Testing Policy
policy.p1: To ensure the fairness and scientific integrity of the leaderboard, ARC Prize enforces a strict testing and verification policy for all submissions.
policy.p2: All leaderboard results are subject to review by our technical team to ensure reproducibility and compliance.
policy.p3: Results marked as "preview" are unofficial and may be based on incomplete testing. Official results are confirmed after full private evaluation.
